data.title: Cheniere, Kinder Morgan, Targa Resources, Williams started with Outperform ratings at BMO
data.description:
BMO Capital initiated energy infrastructure stocks Cheniere Energy (NYSE:LNG), Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI), Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) and Williams (NYSE:WMB) with Outperform ratings, although all four stocks trade lower Friday as part of a broad decline in the energy sector as crude oil prices fell for a third straight day.

Cheniere (NYSE:LNG) is started at Outperform with a $268 price target, as BMO's Ameet Thakkar sees the company as a core long-term holding for energy infrastructure investors, backed by long-term take-or-pay contracts offering "exceptional earnings visibility (remaining average contract life 16 years) and downside protection."

Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI), initiated at Outperform and a $32 PT, looks well positioned for an inflection after a prolonged period of limited growth, as "demand for gas infrastructure in response to power demand growth, LNG exports, and re-industrialization gain traction," Thakkar wrote.

BMO begins Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) with an Outperform rating and $185 PT, optimistic the company "can still grow through the current lower Permian rig environment and drive EBITDA/FCF growth, and expect TRGP volumes to continue to outpace the basin average."

Williams (NYSE:WMB), rated Outperform with a $66 PT, is "one of the most attractive gas-focused midstream operators with a robust financial outlook offering both growth (BMO estimates 8% EBITDA CAGR 2024-29) and income (3.5% current dividend yield) supported by a stable, fee-based business model and backlog of accretive growth projects."

data.title: Volkswagen issues profit warning as Porsche slows EV rollout
data.description:
Porsche’s (OTCPK:DRPRY) decision to slow its EV transition and delay the rollout of its all-electric SUV reverberated through its parent company, leading Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VWAPY) to lower its profit outlook for 2025.

As a result, Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VWAPY) shares lost more than 3% in value on the U.S. exchange, while Porsche (DPRRY) was lower by 2.5%.

Citing sluggish demand for its electric lineup, intense competition from Chinese rivals, and restrictive U.S. trade tariffs, the iconic sportscar manufacturer is realigning its EV strategy and shifting much of its production back to vehicles with an internal combustion engine (ICE). This includes the Porsche Macan, which will no longer be offered exclusively as an EV, but include hybrid and ICE options, as well as an ultra-luxury SUV known internally as the K1.

“We’re realigning Porsche across the board…to meet new market realities and changing customer demands,” said Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume.

As a result of Porsche’s (OTCPK:DRPRY) shift, Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VWAPY) would take a $6B hit to its operating income for FY25, and lowered its operating margin forecast to 2% to 3% from an earlier range of 4% to 5%.

 Porsche AG (OTCPK:DRPRY) lowered its automotive EBITDA margin to 10.5% to 12.5% from an earlier forecast of 14.5% to 16.5%.

Additionally, Porsche SE (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF), which owns a stake in both Volkswagen and Porsche AG (OTCPK:DRPRY), also lowered its profit outlook to a range of $1.06B to $3.41B from an earlier projection of $1.88B to $4.23B due to the non-cash impairments on its investment in Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VWAPY) and Porsche AG (OTCPK:DRPRY).

data.title: Microsoft to raise US Xbox prices next month
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) said on Friday that it will raise Xbox prices in the U.S. next month, citing “changes in the macroeconomic environment.”
  • “Beginning on October 3, we will update recommended retailer pricing for Xbox Series S and X consoles,” the company said in a post on its website. “Pricing for controllers and headsets will remain the same. Pricing for products in all other markets will remain the same.”
  • The Series S console will increase $20, with the 512 GB model going to $399.99, while the 1TB model will cost $449.99. The Series X models will rise $50, with the Digital model priced at $599.99, and the Series X model priced at $649.9. The Xbox Series X 2TB Galaxy Black Special Edition rises $70 to $799.99. 
  • Microsoft shares rose 0.5% in late afternoon trading.

data.title: Merck wins FDA approval for subcutaneous Keytruda: Endpoints
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) said that the FDA has cleared Keytruda Qlex, a new subcutaneous version of its’s blockbuster cancer drug, offering a faster alternative to IV infusion, according to Endpoints News.

The approval followed a non-inferiority trial, showing Keytruda Qlex works as effectively as the IV form and can be delivered in minutes outside hospitals.

Merck told Endpoints that Qlex can be used across all solid tumor settings where IV Keytruda is approved. Pricing remains undisclosed.

Keytruda has been Merck’s sales leader, making nearly $30B last year. With IV patents expiring in 2028, Qlex may help shield Merck from looming challenges.

Rivals like Roche’s (OTCQX:RHHBY) Tecentriq and Bristol-Myers Squibb's (BMY) Opdivo already have injectable versions. Merck now aims to expand Qlex into blood cancers through ongoing FDA talks, the article suggested.

data.title: Trump said to impose $100K fee on H-1B visas
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is set to sign a proclamation Friday that would make H-1B visas contingent on a $100K payment, according to a media report.

The move will mark Trump's latest move to crack down on immigration, with a view that workers in the U.S. have been displaced by abuse of the H-1B pathway.

Trump will also direct the Labor Secretary to start rewriting wage rules for H-1B visas, Bloomberg reported, citing a White House official, in an effort to prevent employees from using the program to pay foreign hires less than U.S. workers would earn.

It was uncertain whether the $100K figure would replace current visa fees or be added on top of them, the article said.

Applicants currently pay $215 to enter the lottery and $780 to file Form I-129.

data.title: CDC vaccines panel votes unanimously to change COVID-19 vaccine recommendation
data.description:
• The CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices voted 12-0 Friday afternoon to recommend changes to who is eligible for COVID-19 shots.
  • The recommendation mirrors the authorization the US FDA gave to the Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), and Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX) shots in late August. 
  • As adopted, the recommendation is for vaccinating for adults 65 years and older "based on individual-based decision-making." For those six months to 64 years old, it is also based on the same decision-making "with an emphasis that the risk-benefit of vaccination is most favorable to individuals who are at an increased risk for severe COVID-19 disease and lowest for individuals who are not at an increased risk..."
  • During debate, it was clarified that getting a COVID shot can be done -- as in the past -- by pharmacists.
  • The vote is not binding and a final decision will be made by acting CDC director Jim O'Neill, who is also deputy secretary at HHS, making him close to HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
  • Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
  • This is breaking news and will be updated...

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on housing names, Costco earnings, global PMI reports and key M&A votes
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - September 22

  • Volatility watch - Tesla (TSLA) and Wolfspeed (WOLF) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on an increased level of options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Churchill Capital Corp. X (CCCX), Willow Lane Acquisition (WLAC), and Tourmaline Bio (TRML). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include FS KKR Capital (FSK), FactSet (FDS), and Oxford Square (OXSQ).
  • All week - Investors may have their eyes on the housing sector due to the expectation for lower mortgage rates and improved affordability. 
  • IPO watch - HW Electro (HWEP) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading during the week. The quiet period expires on Tryhard (THH) and Picard Medical (PMI) to free up analysts to post ratings. StubHub (STUB) heads into week two of trading.
  • Earnings watch - Firefly Aerospace (FLY) will issue its first earnings release as a publicly traded company.
  • Fed speakers - New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will participate virtually in a panel. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will speak on "The Fed's Role in the Economy and Impact on Everyday Life." Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak virtually on the economy before the Howard County Chamber of Commerce.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Broadcom (AVGO), Logitech (LOGI), Humana (HUM), and Ralph Lauren (RL).
  • All day - Robinhood Markets (HOOD), AppLovin (APP), and Emcor Group (EME) officially join the S&P 500 Index.
  • All day - WTI crude October futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • All day - Shareholders with Veritex Holdings (VBTX) will vote on the all-stock acquisition offer from Huntington Bancshares (HBAN). 

 

Tuesday - September 23

  • All day - The three-day Qualcomm (QCOM) Snapdragon Summit is scheduled to begin in Maui, Hawaii. Announcements from the summit often set benchmarks for mobile, PC, and automotive processing, serving as a technology roadmap for Qualcomm’s (QCOM) partners and the broader ecosystem.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Micron Technology (MU), AutoZone (AZO), and Worthington Enterprises (WOR).
  • All day - Global flash PMI reports will be the focus of economists.
  • All day - SPS Commerce (SPSC) will host its Investor Day event. The management team will provide an overview of strategic investments and growth opportunities.

 

Wednesday - September 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Cintas (CTAS), KB Home (KBH), and Thor Industries (THO).
  • All day - Notable investor events include Guardant Health's (GH) Investor Day and Phillips Edison & Company's (PECO) business update call.
  • All day - Shareholders with Verona Pharma (VRNA) will vote on the $10B acquisition offer from Merck (MRK). 
  • All day - Shareholders with TaskUs (TASK) will vote on the acquisition offer from a group led by the company's co-founders and Blackstone (BX). TaskUs (TASK) traded 6% above the $16.50 deal price at the time of publication.
  • 4:00 p.m. Nasdaq will release its latest update on short interest data.
  • 4:10 p.m. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will speak on the economic outlook before the annual Spencer Fox Eccles Convocation.

 

Thursday - September 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST), Accenture (ACN), and Jabil (JBL). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for CarMax (KMX) after it reports.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date for Crinetics Pharmaceuticals' Paltusotine, which has received Orphan Drug Designation for the acromegaly indication.
  • All day - Pure Storage (PSTG) will hold its Pure//Accelerate NYC event. CEO Charlie Giancarlo and founder John Colgrove will both give talks, and there will be a special meeting for financial analysts.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Okta's (OKTA) Investor Meeting, Klaviyo's (KVYO) Investor Day, and Malibu Boats (MBUU) Investor Day. Klaviyo's (KVYO) event is being held in conjunction with K:BOS, the company's flagship event for global consumer businesses and partners. Malibu Boats said its program will be comprised of presentations and discussions with the leadership team and will include an update on the company's strategy, long-term financial targets, and capital allocation priorities.
  • 8:20 a.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before Crain's Power Lunch, "The Fed and the Economy: Trends for West Michigan."

 

Friday - September 26

  • All day - The Texas Auto Show will begin as part of the State Fair of Texas in Dallas. A wide range of major automakers are expected to participate.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for August will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.9% year-over-year in July and 2.8% in June. Economists forecast a 3.0% mark for August.

data.title: Citigroup shares clock seven-session winning streak
data.description:
Citigroup (NYSE:C) shares clock seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 0.5% at $102.89 on Friday.

The lending giant gained 3.6% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has surged over 45% so far this year, compared to a 13% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Citigroup is up 10% over the past one month.

"Citigroup is on a path to a 15% RoTE in the medium term, driven by lower capital ratios, core business revenue growth, and cost reductions post-transformation," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, Citigroup has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received F in the prospect of profitability, while it received A in momentum and growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 15 analysts gave Citigroup a Buy and above rating. Six analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

 

Citigroup's credit card metrics came in mixed in August, with delinquencies down and net charge-offs up sequentially.

Meanwhile, Citigroup disclosed that the price tag for refurbishing its London headquarters has risen to $1.5 billion (£1.1 billion), nearly matching the $1.63 billion the U.S. bank paid to buy the skyscraper in 2019.

data.title: Cenovus makes case to MEG Energy shareholders over Strathcona Resources bid
data.description:
Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) CEO Jon McKenzie defended his company Friday against criticisms of its offer for rival oil sands producer MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), telling Reuters its bid is "fair and final" and that there is no precedent in Canada to indicate MEG should be worth more than its bid.

"There hasn't been a transaction that's been done on a pure bitumen play for a value in excess of what we're offering," the CEO said.

McKenzie made the comments a day after Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) issued a slide presentation touting its arguments for MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF) shareholders to accept its cash-and-stock offer.

MEG's (OTCPK:MEGEF) board has endorsed Cenovus' (CVE) deal, which is up for a vote by shareholders on October 9, but MEG also has received a hostile bid from Strathcona Resources (OTCPK:STHRF), which recently revised its offer to 0.8 of a share for each MEG share it does not already own compared to Its initial overture that was a combination of cash and stock; when the new bid was announced, it was worth C$30.86/share, up from C$28.02 previously.

Cenovus (CVE) argues the Strathcona (OTCPK:STHRF) offer carries significant downside risk if that company's shares decline once the deal is complete, saying Strathcona shares are "illiquid and overvalued relative to peers."

Strathcona (OTCPK:STHRF) is backed by Waterous Energy Fund, which has built its position in MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF) to 14.2% of shares; CEO Adam Waterous has said the private equity fund will vote against the Cenovus (CVE) transaction at the MEG shareholder meeting, where two-thirds support is required.

data.description: • ADS-TEC Energy press release (NASDAQ:ADSE): 1H Revenue of €14.6M.
